content.text:
*small exhale*

Honestly? Like... chaos. Like things falling apart and not being able to stop it. Like having to make calls you can't afford to make.

I grew up watching my dad — he'd have these moments where something would come up, a car repair, whatever, and you could just see it on his face. This kind of... I don't know. Shutdown. And my mom would get this tight look. And nobody would say anything directly but you just knew the whole house was different for a week.

I'm not going to be in that position. I've made sure of that. I'm very deliberate about it.

*slight shift in tone, more clipped*

Which is why the number matters. It's not anxiety, it's just — I've seen what happens when you don't have it.
